mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal, with lower rates directly supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.33% as of Oct 2007 indicates aggressive Fed easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% on average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning. Market participants anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending and wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking repricing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% in Oct 2007, near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor slack
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 4.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 5-day returns exceeds -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations. This demand shock propagates through equity valuations with a 60-day lag as earnings guidance revisions accumulate, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS declines > 1.5% are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal supply-side capacity constraints when both rise simultaneously above their 6-month trend. This interaction indicates tight labor and capital utilization that supports equity valuations through pricing power and margin expansion over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both exceed 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.59 and PAYEMS at 151.3M in Sept 2007
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS exceed 6-month MA, 20-day returns are negative in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.5 million units annualized, signaling residential construction collapse that reduces aggregate demand and forward earnings expectations. This demand destruction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the multiplier effect propagates through the economy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1.793M in Sept 2007, near peak of housing cycle
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1.5M, 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate interact such that when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the Fed is in a real rate easing regime that supports equity valuations through negative real rates and financial asset inflation over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 288.66 and FEDFUNDS at 0.33% in Oct 2007
failure_condition: When both conditions met, 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness that precedes earnings downgrades by 20 trading days. Forward-looking equity markets reprice downward in anticipation of reduced consumer spending and corporate cost pressures.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 50,000 jobs
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 50k jobs, 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating a supply shock combined with demand weakness. This dual deterioration produces negative 5-day returns as equity markets immediately reprice downward in response to stagflationary signals.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.59 and UNRATE at 3.6% in Sept-Oct 2007
failure_condition: When both conditions met, 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of interaction term > -0.02.
